# PDF-Only Preview Architecture Design

**Goal:** Replace the "Ao Vivo" React rendering stack with the existing PDF preview pipeline, eliminating schema-coupled frontend rendering entirely.

**Architecture:** The preview panel always shows the real PDF generated by Carbone, stored in MinIO, and rendered by react-pdf. The "Ao Vivo" / "PDF" tab distinction is removed. After each auto-save (which already triggers `renderDocumentPDF()` synchronously), the frontend refreshes the signed URL and the preview updates. All DynamicPreview/PreviewTemplate React components are deleted.

**Tech Stack:** Carbone (existing), MinIO (existing), react-pdf/pdfjs-dist (existing `PdfPreview.tsx`), Go backend (no changes).

---

## Problem

The current "Ao Vivo" tab renders a React component tree (`DynamicPreview` -> `ScalarField`/`TableField`/etc. in "preview" mode) that duplicates the Carbone template rendering. This creates:

1. **Two rendering paths** for the same document — React preview vs. Carbone PDF — that diverge visually.
2. **Schema coupling** — every new document type needs React preview components maintained alongside the Carbone template.
3. **Overflow/layout bugs** — the React rendering at zoom scale doesn't handle tables and long text correctly.
4. **~15 files** of preview-specific React code that add maintenance cost with zero unique value.

## Solution

### What gets deleted

All files in the "Ao Vivo" React rendering stack:

- `frontend/apps/web/src/features/documents/runtime/DynamicPreview.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/DocumentPreviewRenderer.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewDocumentPage.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewSectionBlock.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewFieldRenderer.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewTextField.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewArrayField.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewChecklistField.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/PreviewTableField.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/templates/PreviewTemplatePO.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/templates/PreviewTemplateIT.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/templates/PreviewTemplateRG.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/templates/PreviewTemplateFM.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/templates/PreviewTemplateGeneric.tsx`
- `frontend/apps/web/src/components/content-builder/preview/templates/templateRegistry.ts`

### CSS to delete

In `frontend/apps/web/src/styles.css`:
- All `.preview-document*` rules (`.preview-document`, `.preview-document-page`, `.preview-document-content`, `.preview-document-footer`)
- All `.preview-doc-*` rules (header table, cells, labels, meta)
- The `@container` query for `.preview-document-page` zoom
- The `.preview-panel-body { container-type: inline-size }` (no longer needed)

In `frontend/apps/web/src/features/documents/runtime/DynamicEditor.module.css`:
- `.previewSectionMode.section` and its nested rules
- `.previewSectionMode .sectionHeader`
- `.previewSectionMode .sectionBadge`
- `.previewValue`
- `.previewEmpty`

### What gets simplified

**`PreviewPanel.tsx`** — rewrite to remove tabs and always show `PdfPreview`:

```
PreviewPanel (simplified)
  props: pdfUrl, isDirty, isSaving, collapsed, onToggleCollapse
  
  if collapsed -> show expand button (unchanged)
  
  otherwise:
    header: collapse button + title "Preview" (no tabs)
    body:
      if !pdfUrl          -> empty state: "Salve o documento para gerar o preview"
      if pdfUrl && isDirty -> PdfPreview + stale badge "Alteracoes nao salvas"
      if pdfUrl && isSaving -> PdfPreview + overlay "Atualizando preview..."
      if pdfUrl && !isDirty && !isSaving -> PdfPreview (clean)
```

Props removed: `schema`, `contentDraft`, `profileCode`, `documentCode`, `documentTitle`, `documentStatus`, `version`, `activeSectionKey`. These only served the live preview rendering.

Props added: `isSaving` (boolean, true while auto-save API call is in flight).

**`ContentBuilderView.tsx`** — remove all props/state that only served the live preview:
- Remove `activeSectionKey` forwarding to preview
- Remove `documentStatus` threading through preview
- Remove `schema` and `contentDraft` passing to PreviewPanel
- Add `isSaving` state (set true when auto-save starts, false when it completes)
- Ensure `pdfUrl` updates after each successful auto-save response

### Loading/stale UX

Four states, never blanking the preview:

| State | Condition | Visual |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| Fresh | `!isDirty && pdfUrl` | PDF rendered, no indicators |
| Stale | `isDirty && pdfUrl` | Current PDF visible + subtle top-right badge "Alteracoes nao salvas" |
| Generating | `isSaving` | Current PDF visible + semi-transparent overlay with spinner + "Atualizando preview..." |
| Empty | `!pdfUrl` | Placeholder with document icon + "Salve o documento para gerar o preview" |

Design rules:
- Old PDF always stays visible during stale/generating states (no flicker)
- Stale badge is a small semi-transparent pill, not alarming
- Generating overlay is light (white at 80% opacity + spinner)
- `PdfPreview.tsx` already handles its internal loading when a new URL is set

### PdfPreview width

`PdfPreview` currently receives a hardcoded `width={380}`. This must change to fill the available panel width dynamically. Use a `ResizeObserver` (or `useRef` + `clientWidth`) on the preview body container to pass the correct width to `PdfPreview`. The PDF pages will scale to fit the panel at any drag position.

### What does NOT change

- Go backend: zero changes. `SaveNativeContentAuthorized()` already generates PDF on every save.
- Carbone templates: untouched.
- MinIO storage: untouched.
- Auto-save debounce logic: untouched.
- Editor components (`DynamicEditor`, `ScalarField` in edit mode, etc.): untouched.
- The `PdfPreview.tsx` component itself: untouched (it already handles loading/error states).

### Backend assumption to verify

The auto-save response (`saveDocumentContentNative`) must return the updated `pdfUrl` (signed MinIO URL). If it currently does, no backend changes are needed. If it does not, the frontend needs to call the `GET /api/v1/documents/{documentID}/content/pdf` endpoint after save to fetch the new signed URL.

## Expected outcome

- Preview panel is ~30 lines of JSX (down from ~15 files / ~500 lines).
- Adding a new document type requires zero frontend preview code.
- What the user sees IS the real document — no visual divergence between preview and export.
- Tables, images, headers, footers all render correctly because Carbone handles them.
- Preview scales to any panel width via react-pdf's width prop.
